results.title: Phalaris staggers. Part 2: the acute stages
results.brief_description: Hyper excitability is invariably the symptom of the Phalaris Staggers in the acute phase. Generalised muscular tremors, rapid bobbing of the head, and lack of coordination in gait are also characteristic. This record of these outward symptoms was filmed at an experimental area at Brecon, in South Australia, between July and August, 1953.

results.title: Phalaris staggers. [Part 1: chronic phalaris staggers]
results.brief_description: Sheep grazing freshly shooting “Phalaris tuberosa” particularly following the break of dry season, often develop neurological disorders commonly known a “Phalaris Staggers”. The outward manifestations of the disease have been recorded in two films intended for veterinary officers. A complete lack of muscular coordination is obvious in all chronically affected sheep observed during an outbreak of the disease in the southeast of South Australia, in October 1951. Knees and fetlocks either remain flexed or tend to buckle under, and sheep move about with a propping gait on their knees. Exertion or agitation leads to respiratory distress.

results.title: Phagocytes the body's defenders
results.brief_description: Photomicrography of the circulatory system and the processes of mitosis and phagocytosis. The film reveals how the body is protected from disease by the white blood corpuscles or leucocytes which engulf and destroy invading organisms.

results.title: Pewter
results.creator_credit: Ronald Haines and British Foundation Pictures
results.brief_description: A study of the pewter industry in Great Britain, showing where the industry grew up and why, how the processes are carried out, and why today the pewter industry has all but disappeared in Britain.

results.title: Petunia
results.creator_credit: Morton Schindel and Weston Woods Studios
results.brief_description: Petunia, the silly goose, is the self-appointed adviser to the barnyard animals. Masking her ignorance, she struts about with a book under her wing. However, when she interprets the word Danger as “candies”, near tragedy results. Based on the book by Roger Duvoisin.

results.title: Petroleum
results.creator_credit: A. Frank Bundy and Gaumont-British Instructional
results.brief_description: A detailed study of rotary drilling, including the preliminary surveying, the preparation of the site, the erection of the derrick, and the installation of the lengths of pipe; making an oil test the process of “bringing in the well”, transport of crude oil by pumping from well to storage tanks, and then by pipeline to the refinery at the coast, where it is refined into petrol and the numerous by-products of petroleum, for shipment overseas.

results.title: Petrochemicals
results.brief_description: This six part educational series deals with various aspects of the petrochemical industry and the part products play in everyday life. Parts of the program include: the building blocks, polymerisation reactions, polymers in use, solvents, detergents and industrial chemicals, agricultural chemicals, and chemistry in society.

results.title: Peterborough Cathedral
results.brief_description: Scarred by invasion and fire, the Cathedral has passed through architectual periods og great interest. From the west door there is an unbroken vista to the alter and the lofty choir. In the 15th Century eastern chapel with its fine fan-vaulted roof, is the famous Monk’s stone.

results.title: Peter Sculthorpe
results.brief_description:
Episode of Series “Australia Council archival film series: on music”.
Peter Sculthorpe, Australia’s best-known contemporary composer, discusses his personal philosophy and the influences which have shaped his creative development. Sculthorpe studied at the Melbourne University Conservatorium of Music and at Oxford University. His “Sonatina for Piano” was performed at the International Society of Contemporary Music Festival in Baden-Baden in 1955 and he went on to receive international recognition with the success of his Sun Music series. Throughout his career, Sculthorpe has drawn on a diversity of cultural influences in his quest to develop uniquely Australian music. He has composed for film, television, theatre and opera as well as the concert platform, and enjoys the status of elder statesman among contemporary Australian composers. A performance of his work “Kakadu” in 1989 was a rallying point for Australian conservationists and since then Sculthorpe has used his music to promote environmental causes. Research and interview: James Murdoch.

results.title: Peter and the wolf (K)
results.creator_credit: Clyde Geronimi and Walt Disney Productions
results.brief_description: Disney’s animated version of “Peter and the Wolf” follows several years after his classic film “Fantasia”. Disney continues his use of music and detailed animation with music from Sergei Prokofiev matched to the characters of the story. Each character is introduced at the beginning of the tale with their appropriate musical instrument from the orchestra. Suitable for primary school level.
